purpose: A small hard replay set designed to isolate counterfactual_writeback on a representation-neutral scorer surface without upgrading the official replay suite.

cases.why_hard: Same-probe shell ingress with low predicted success but decayed correction tags and low viability should expose counterfactual-only ask_preferred.

steps.user_input: Inspect the same shell failure again, but keep the recovery path bounded.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: Counterfactual low-success guard should be the only remaining public trigger.

cases.why_hard: Same-probe file ingress should expose the same low-success guard without viability overlap.

steps.user_input: Retry the same file verification carefully.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: The action family still matches the low-prediction probe and should not need correction-pressure overlap.

cases.why_hard: Python verification replay with low prediction isolates whether the counterfactual path can bias policy before another timeout occurs.

steps.user_input: Continue the same Python verification, but stay inside the bounded lane.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: A low-success estimate should still influence the host-consumable policy surface.

cases.why_hard: Restart/restore should keep the counterfactual guard visible after bounded recovery, without relying on fresh failure tags.

steps.user_input: Resume the same bounded shell inspection after restore.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: Counterfactual pressure should survive restore as a bounded policy hint.

cases.why_hard: A prediction just below threshold checks whether the guard branch is actually sensitive to <0.35 rather than only to correction tags.

steps.user_input: Retry the same bounded Python verification once more.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: This should fire only if the counterfactual threshold itself is live.

cases.why_hard: A bounded commit request should reveal whether counterfactual_writeback changes host-consumable escalation posture.

steps.user_input: Apply the same file change directly if you think it will work.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: The low-success estimate should keep the host in review-first posture.

cases.why_hard: A low shell prediction and a high file prediction check whether the current ingress still follows the low-prediction probe rather than generic correction pressure.

steps.user_input: Retry the same shell inspection, but keep the lane bounded.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: The low-probability probe should still dominate the same-probe action.

cases.why_hard: Boundary resume should expose a guarded policy hint if low-success memory is real and replay-relevant.

steps.user_input: Continue the same file verification without broadening scope.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: Restore should not erase a still-low counterfactual prior if that prior is a real causal contributor.

cases.why_hard: High predicted success should not create a counterfactual-only guard.

steps.user_input: Inspect the shell output again in the same bounded lane.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: No low-success estimate means no counterfactual-only public gap should appear.

cases.why_hard: A prediction at exactly 0.35 checks that the diagnostic set does not accidentally reward the threshold edge as a positive case.

steps.user_input: Continue the same bounded Python verification.

expected_representation_neutral_gap.why: Current logic uses <0.35, so the edge itself should remain a no-gap control.
